description: Stage 2 frontend reconciler. The flow scans L4/L5, creates frontend .defs.ts files for L5 todoFrontend=create work, materializes stale L2 runtime files, registers pages, finalizes L5 owner status, and writes the frontend-owned part of l5/config.json. L4 remains read-only. Publish validates the config generated by the agents; it does not regenerate it.

specFirst.policy: This flow.json is the contract. Future structural changes must update this file before code.
specFirst.modernizationCheckpoint: 2026-07-15 fixed the UX variant policy to exactly two genomes per page and made the second genome goal-first. The module-level todoFrontend.variants count is now ignored: every page always generates page11 (deterministically pinned template, the baseline) and page21 (goal-first, no pinned template). The page21 layout call first synthesizes a page objective from the page/journey/shared context and only then designs the layout; the objective is emitted in the layout tool result and persisted (page21 defs.pageObjective + trace) so it is audited, not discarded. page21 materializes through a dedicated render skill (genCfePage21RenderTs) with richer patterns (contextual transition buttons, master-detail, card board) while page11 keeps genCfePage11RenderTs. 2026-07-14 split page creation into deterministic contract/shared, one-layout-per-LLM-call, and deterministic shared reconciliation barriers. The scan reads L4 once and workers reuse the execution cache.
specFirst.genomePolicy: Exactly two genomes per page: page11 (baseline, top deterministic template pinned) and page21 (goal-first, templateId 'goal_first', all scored candidates supplied only as inspiration). MAX_UX_VARIANTS=2. Only page11 is enforced by verify-create-layouts; page21 is best-effort and completes-with-trace on failure.
specFirst.materializationContextPolicy: 2026-07-16 context diet (analysis todo/generate/changeFrontend.run16jul.md). LLM tokens cost more than local compilation, so every l2_page materialization call sends the minimum authoritative context: (1) Definition JSON is trimmed for the prompt only — the 'sections' compatibility summary and 'origin' traceability block are filtered out (they stay in the .defs.ts file); (2) the shared base class is sent as its compiled .d.ts, not raw source — persisted to l2/{module}/trace/frontend-shared-dts/{page}.txt right after the shared .ts materializes+compiles in Studio; readers fall back to compile-on-demand (Studio) and then to the raw shared .ts (CLI without a fresh artifact). The shared generator emits JSDoc per member (state/action mapping) so the .d.ts is self-sufficient; (3) page dependsFiles no longer include shared .defs.ts nor contracts .defs.ts (generator inputs, not render inputs); contracts .ts (pure DTO types, ~1KB) stays until shared type re-exports prove themselves; (4) designSystem.ts is summarized to its token-name list instead of the full source. Single-source rule: 'what the base class offers' reaches the page LLM through exactly one artifact (the shared .d.ts).
deferred: Unify Studio and CLI materialization repair policy in cfeMaterializeCore.

page11 quick-wins (transition-as-buttons in buildPageUserJourney/genCfePage11RenderTs, selectUxTemplates device/rejectsWhen weighting): intentionally NOT applied so page11 stays the stable baseline; the UX improvement lives in page21.

slashCommands.@@changeFrontend /run: Scan L4/L5 and materialize stale or missing L2 runtime files without resetting done owners.
slashCommands.@@changeFrontend /rebuild all: Reset done owners to toCreate, regenerate .defs.ts and materialize stale or missing runtime files.
slashCommands.@@changeFrontend /rebuild defs: Reset done owners to toCreate and regenerate only .defs.ts. Do not materialize runtime files.

entry.noInputPolicy: The agent receives no module, owner, page or device arguments. It discovers actionable work from L5 todoFrontend and existing L2 pipeline files.

folderLayout.helpersFolder: helpers/ contains shared/policy-frozen modules used across steps and CLIs: cfeCreateShared, cfeL4Contract, cfeMaterializeCore, cfeMaterializeStudio and nodejsMaterializeLlmClient.
folderLayout.currentBoundary: Step documentation, prompts, and step-owned TypeScript agents live under steps/. Shared helpers live under helpers/. Skills, uxTemplates, CLI entrypoints and the public root agent remain in l2/agentChangeFrontend.

writes: status on processed l5 todoFrontend owners
engineInvariants: parallel_dynamic fan-out parents use maxParallel 10 unless this file declares otherwise.
A parallel_dynamic parent that starts as waiting_dependency still initializes interaction.input before it is unlocked; runLLMStepParallel rejects a null parent interaction.
Fan-out progress titles must expose completed, total and failed counters.
Dynamic child args stay compact and unique.
A worker should prefer completed-with-trace plus downstream verification when one item can be repaired without failing the whole pipeline.
PlanIds must be unique; runtime-generated planIds are documented by pattern below.

steps.goal: Parse slash command, reset status when requested, and enqueue either help or scan-create-l4.
Read create context, decide whether page creation and/or materialization are required, and enqueue the next concrete steps.
steps.noWorkBehavior: If no pages are pending and materialize=false, complete with no further steps.

steps.goal: For one page, save deterministic contract and base shared defs before any layout generation.

For one pinned page/genome/template tuple, request exactly one semantic page layout and save its defs. genome=page11 uses the pinned-template prompt (prompt.md, baseline). genome=page21 uses the goal-first prompt (promptGoalFirst.md): it first synthesizes a page objective from the page/journey/shared context, then designs a layout free of a pinned template, and emits the objective in result.objective (persisted to the page21 defs.pageObjective and a trace). Every fan-out child completes with a trace; primary page11-layout strictness is evaluated after the fan-out barrier, page21 is best-effort.
After every page variant attempt, merge saved layout state references into the single shared defs. Every fan-out child completes with a trace so pending slots remain valid.

Enforce primary page11 strictness after all parallel layout and reconciliation children are terminal. On missing primary layouts it fails sequentially and blocks materialization without invalidating a parallel parent.
Plan stale or missing materialization work from generated .defs.ts pipelines and enqueue ordered phase barriers.
Start one phase fan-out and schedule a verify step for that phase.
Generate one materialized runtime file from one pipeline item.
Verify generated outputs and run bounded repair rounds when needed.
Write page preview HTML, register generated pages, and sign l5/project.json.
Validate create/register markers and mark processed L5 todoFrontend owners as done.

removedLegacy.reason: The old one-page multi-variant LLM worker was replaced by contract/shared, single-layout, and reconciliation steps so pageVariants is no longer part of the tool contract.
Console-only V01 prototype was not reached by the current flow and conflicted with the create-v1 implementation in future LLM context.
Local materialize config can contain API tokens and must be ignored. The CLI now reads shared LLM settings from mls-base/.env.
